title: pavonia latibracteolata
intro: Pavonia latibracteolata, also known as the Mexican Rosemallow, is a plant species belonging to the mallow family (Malvaceae) that is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its ability to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Pavonia latibracteolata include reducing inflammation, soothing wounds, and treating gastrointestinal issues, among others. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in topical applications, such as salves or creams, to harness its medicinal properties.
